<!-- Generated by the multi-agent design sprint (run wf_d7ec2546-6b5) on 2026-06-18.
     5 divergent directions -> 4 perspective-diverse judges -> synthesis -> completeness critic.
     This is a proposal/RFC for the post-v0.2.0 ground-up dashboard redesign, not yet implemented. -->

# RFC: SwissKnife Dashboard Ground-Up Redesign — The Adaptive Money Plane

**Status:** Proposed (v2 — corrected against backend reality) · **Author:** Design sprint + human review · **Target stack:** Next.js 16 / React 19 / MUI v9 (Minimal template fork) · **Audience:** SwissKnife frontend + backend

---

## Revision note (v2)

The first draft (sprint synthesis) made factual errors about the backend, now corrected by the maintainer:

- **On-chain send IS supported** — the `SendPaymentRequest.input` doc string saying otherwise is stale and should be removed. The unified Send routes both rails today; there is no "coming soon" dead-path.
- **The fused BIP21 receive QR is frontend-composable today** — `bitcoin:<addr>?amount=…&lightning=<bolt11>` is display-only, built from the on-chain address + bolt11 we already hold. It is **not** backend-blocked and ships in Phase 1.
- **Balance is single-network** — the connected node defines the network (regtest node → regtest balance), so the live balance is one number; there is no cross-network summing to guard against. The earlier "per-network, never summed" machinery is dropped.
- **Spending policy / RBAC budgets are genuinely missing but are v0.3.0 AI-agent scope** — expected future work, not a surprise gap.
- **Don't treat missing backend as a wall** — where a surface needs new backend, it's an implementation task, not a reason to fence. Reserve `flag`s for v0.3.0 surfaces only. (And don't open issues blindly — some may already exist.)

ASCII wireframes below are illustrative and may still show first-draft phrasing; this note and the section prose govern. The auto-generated critique (Appendix A) is annotated for which items these corrections supersede.

---

## 0. TL;DR

We adopt **Direction 5 (Adaptive Shell / composition system) as the structural spine**, because it is the only direction that treats the brief's hardest constraint — *one app must adapt to every persona × deployment mode* — as the deliverable rather than a feature. It is also a *minimal, verified generalization of code that already exists* (`hasAllPermissions()` + permission-tagged `navData`).

Onto that spine we graft the consensus-winning **money UX from Direction 1** (the "rail-on-three-triggers" law, "Spendable now / Arriving soon", one balance, unified Activity), the **operator surfaces from Direction 2** (scope switcher, "Needs Action" pane), the **developer/agent primitives from Direction 4** (agent = wallet + key + policy + budget, the Freeze kill-switch, "Copy as cURL/bolt402"), and the **command-as-API-mirror idea from Direction 3** as a layer, not the IA.

All four judges ranked D1 or D5 at the top; both money-UX laws and the composition engine are the cross-cutting consensus. This RFC is the union of those, with every aspirational feature explicitly fenced off behind verified backend reality.

### Verified ground truth (I read the code; this drives every "ship now vs later" call)

| Claim | Verdict | Evidence |
|---|---|---|
| Invoice carries both rails | **True** | `types.gen.ts:340` `bitcoin_output?`, `:369` `ln_invoice?` |
| One fused BIP21 QR shippable today | **TRUE (frontend-composed)** | display-only: compose `bitcoin:<addr>?amount=…&lightning=<bolt11>` on the client from the on-chain address + the bolt11 we already have. No backend leg needed. _(v2 correction)_ |
| Unified Send routes on-chain | **TRUE** | on-chain send IS supported; the `SendPaymentRequest.input` doc string ("…not yet supported") is **stale** — remove it backend-side. _(v2 correction)_ |
| On-chain receive (address) shippable | **True** | `BtcAddress.used:boolean` (`:98`); generate/list/delete BTC address endpoints exist |
| Per-account RBAC roles / spending policy / approvals | **FALSE — by design, v0.3.0** | `Permission` (`:742`) is a flat scope enum today; spending policy/budgets are scoped to the v0.3.0 AI-agent work, not a surprise gap |
| Permission-gated nav engine exists | **True** | `hasAllPermissions()` (`auth/permissions.ts`), `navData` items carry `permissions?: string[]` |
| Scope switcher is mock | **True** | `workspaces-popover.tsx:35` `useState(data[0])`, hardcoded `plan` Label |
| Node view is analytics, not health | **True** | `node-view.tsx` renders only volume; no channel/liquidity/peer data |

**Design law for the whole RFC (v2):** design for the *real target product*; where the backend is missing something non-trivial, treat it as an implementation task (the team can add it), not a wall. Reserve manifest `flag`s for genuinely *future* surfaces (the v0.3.0 agent/policy/L402/webhook work), which still degrade gracefully when absent. On-chain send and the fused-QR receive are **real, shippable flows** — not fenced.

---

## 1. Vision and design principles

### 1.1 The mental model shift

The current dashboard is **two apps wearing one theme**: a `WALLET` group and an `ADMINISTRATION` group, each with its own Payments and Invoices, a Total-vs-Current double balance, Lightning-only "Receive Bitcoin," and modals that duplicate inline cards. It renders identically regardless of who logs in or how it's deployed.

The redesign reframes SwissKnife as **a manifest of capabilities rendered against a `(mode × role × scope)` tuple.** There are no persona-specific apps. There is one renderer; the operator, the cashier, the developer, and (v0.3.0) the AI agent are different *resolutions* of the same composition engine. This is the only model that satisfies "one app, all modes" without forking, and it is built directly on the `hasAllPermissions` + `navData.permissions` pattern already in the tree.

### 1.2 The seven principles

1. **One money, many rails — the rail is a derived property, never a primary input.** Codified as a hard, testable law (§4): *the rail surfaces on exactly three triggers — fee, speed/finality, and address reuse — and nowhere else.* This is the single highest-consensus idea across all four judges; adopt it verbatim.
2. **Flows are surfaces, not modals.** Send/Receive/row-detail become drawers (desktop) / full-screen sheets (mobile), reusing the established settings/account/notifications drawer pattern. Every modal in `components/transactions/*-dialog.tsx` is deleted.
3. **Adaptation is the product.** Modules declare `requires[]` (permissions) and `modes[]` (deployment). The shell renders the runtime union. **Two governing laws:** *areas are stable and never reorder* (muscle memory survives a promotion), and *empty areas render nothing* — no greyed-out "upgrade" chrome, so a cashier's 4-item app feels bespoke, not crippled.
4. **Merge duplicate surfaces.** One balance, one unified **Activity** ledger (replacing 4 nav items), one **Identity hub** (LN address + Nostr + on-chain addresses).
5. **Node = observability, never management.** Read-only health (liquidity, connectivity, channel *summary*, alerts) absorbs today's volume analytics. Never channel management — node-agnosticism is the moat.
6. **Accounts are the product.** Segregation + RBAC + (future) spending policy as first-class governance, designed so the same control governs a child's allowance and an agent's budget.
7. **The dashboard is a typed front-end to the API.** Every executed action can reveal its request as cURL / SDK / bolt402 snippet (collapsed by default, mode-weighted down outside server mode). Using the app teaches the API — the cleanest on-ramp to agents-as-accounts.

### 1.3 What we keep

The visual language — dark theme, MUI v9 tokens, cards, MUI X DataGrid, apexcharts, drawers, QR, the Command-K dialog, `number-input`, `SatsWithIcon`. We reimagine IA, flows, and mental model; we do not touch the visual craft.

---

## 2. Target information architecture

### 2.1 The composition primitive

Generalize today's `navData` array into a typed **module manifest**. `hasAllPermissions()` stays the gate; `mode` adds a second, orthogonal filter.

```ts
type Module = {
  id: string;
  title: string;            // i18n key
  icon: string;
  area: 'money' | 'identity' | 'accounts' | 'observe' | 'build' | 'system';
  requires: Permission[];   // ALL must hold (hasAllPermissions) — the EXISTING gate
  modes: Mode[] | 'all';    // deployment modes that include it
  scope: 'active-account' | 'instance';
  surface: 'page' | 'flow' | 'panel'; // route | drawer | home widget
  weight: number;           // home prominence + nav order
  minRender: 'full' | 'compact';
  flag?: 'agents' | 'policy' | 'webhooks' | 'l402' | 'onchain-send'; // hidden until backend lands
};

type Mode = 'server' | 'umbrel' | 'merchant' | 'desktop' | 'agent';
// Mode = NEXT_PUBLIC_DEPLOYMENT_MODE, install-time, default 'server'
// (idiomatic — global-config already uses NEXT_PUBLIC_* with defaults)
```

`flag` gates v0.3.0/aspirational modules off until the corresponding backend exists, so the shell is **honest by construction** — a wrong `requires[]` can't leak admin to a cashier, and a missing backend can't surface a dead screen.

### 2.2 The stable areas and their modules

```
SwissKnife — Adaptive Money Plane
│  Top bar: [⊞ Scope ▾]  ⌘K Pay, request, or search…  [▭ scan]  [🔔]  [◑]  [avatar ▾]  [net: signet]
│
├─ MONEY                                   scope: active-account
│   ├─ Home / Overview        [page]  modes:all                 requires: —
│   ├─ Send                   [flow]  modes:all                 requires: write:transaction
│   ├─ Receive                [flow]  modes:all                 requires: write:transaction
│   └─ Activity (unified)     [page]  modes:all                 requires: read:transaction
│
├─ IDENTITY                                scope: active-account
│   └─ Identity Hub           [page]  modes:all                 requires: read:ln_address
│        tabs (each gated): Lightning Address · Nostr (NIP-05) ·
│        On-chain addresses (read:btc_address) · Contacts (read:transaction)
│
├─ ACCOUNTS                                scope: instance   (mode renames the noun)
│   ├─ Accounts directory     [page]  modes:server,umbrel,merchant   requires: read:wallet
│   │     label: server "Accounts" · umbrel "Family & members" · merchant "Stores & cashiers"
│   ├─ Roles & access         [page]  modes:server,merchant          requires: read:wallet
│   └─ Spending policies      [page]  modes:server,agent  flag:policy requires: write:wallet
│
├─ OBSERVE                                 scope: instance   (read-only)
│   ├─ Node health            [page]  modes:server,umbrel,desktop    requires: read:ln_node
│   └─ Volume & reconciliation[page]  modes:server,merchant          requires: read:transaction
│
├─ BUILD                                   scope: instance / active-account
│   ├─ API keys               [page]  modes:server,desktop,agent     requires: read:api_key
│   ├─ Agents                 [page]  modes:agent,server  flag:agents requires: write:api_key, write:wallet
│   ├─ Webhooks               [page]  modes:server,agent  flag:webhooks requires: read:api_key
│   └─ L402 endpoints         [page]  modes:server,agent  flag:l402   requires: read:api_key
│
└─ SYSTEM                                  scope: instance
    ├─ Settings (drawer)      [panel] modes:all                 requires: —
    └─ Instance & connection  [page]  modes:server,umbrel,desktop requires: read:ln_node
```

**What this deletes vs today:** the two-group `WALLET`/`ADMINISTRATION` split; two `payments` + two `invoices` items → one **Activity**; `lightning-address` + `nostr-address` + on-chain → one **Identity Hub**; `Total balance` + `Current Balance` → one balance; `Node Management` (analytics) → `Node health` (observability) + `Volume`.

### 2.3 Scope, not duplication

The wallet-vs-admin duplication is cured **structurally** by a single **scope switcher** in the top bar (D2's elegant move — built on the now-real `workspaces-popover`, with its mock `plan` Label repurposed to the account-role badge):

- **Org scope** (requires `read:wallet`): every `instance`-scoped module aggregates across accounts.
- **Account scope**: the entire shell drills into one wallet's context.

**Balance is single-network (v2):** the connected node defines the active network, so the live balance is one number for that network; Org scope sums *across accounts on the same network* (safe). The top-bar network badge stays visible. Only *historical* records that may span networks need currency-scoped care — never the live balance.

### 2.4 Role × deployment-mode adaptation matrix

Rows = modules. Columns = resolved persona. ● = full · ◐ = compact/read-only · ○ = absent (no header).

| Module | Operator (server, all perms) | Sub-account holder (read/write:transaction, read:wallet) | Developer (api_key + read:wallet/tx) | Agent (mode=agent, key-auth) |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Home / Overview | ● cockpit grid | ● simple (balance + 2 verbs) | ● BUILD panels rise | ◐ generated console |
| Send / Receive | ● | ● | ● | ○ (acts via API) |
| Activity | ● Org+Account, 3 lenses | ● single-account, Money lens | ● Requests lens up | ◐ read-only spend log |
| Identity Hub | ● all tabs | ● LN-addr + on-chain | ● | ○ |
| Accounts directory | ● | ○ | ◐ read-only | ○ |
| Roles & access | ● | ○ | ◐ | ○ |
| Spending policies `flag` | ● | ○ | ◐ | ◐ own policy |
| Node health | ● | ○ | ○ | ○ |
| Volume & reconciliation | ● | ○ | ○ | ○ |
| API keys | ● | ○ | ● foregrounded | ◐ own key |
| Agents / Webhooks / L402 `flag` | ● | ○ | ● | ◐ own |
| Settings | ● | ◐ theme/lang/cur | ● | ○ |

**Deployment-mode column (vocabulary + module existence, orthogonal to role):**

| | k8s / Professional (server) | Umbrel (home) | Merchant | Desktop |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| ACCOUNTS label | "Accounts" | "Family & members" | "Stores & cashiers" | hidden if 1 wallet |
| BUILD area | full | API keys only | API keys (plugin) | full |
| OBSERVE | full + reconciliation | Node health (own node) | Volume → "Settlements" | full |
| Receive default | drawer | drawer | full-screen numpad / POS | drawer; ⌘K + scan promoted |
| Send verb | "Send" | "Send" | "Pay out" | "Send" |
| Receive verb | "Receive" | "Receive" | "Charge" | "Receive" |
| Notes | split-container; dashboard is pure API client | head-of-household provisioning | per-till wallets | OS window chrome; global hotkey ⌘K |

The same `WalletView`/Home component maps over resolved `panel`-modules into the existing MUI Grid — **one Home component, not five.**

### 2.5 The "why is this hidden?" inspector

A fully data-driven shell makes "why can't I see X?" a function of three axes. **Mandatory** (all judges flagged it): an operator-only dev affordance (gated to `read:wallet` + `?debug=modules`) that lists every module and, per module, which gate failed (`requires` missing perm / `mode` excluded / `flag` off). This is the support-cost insurance that makes the engine maintainable.

---

## 3. Screen-by-screen design

### 3.1 Home / Overview — adaptive panel grid

**Operator · server mode** (cockpit resolution). The grid is data-driven: panels are `surface:'panel'` modules ordered by `weight × role-affinity`.

```
┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ [⊞ Org ▾]   ⌘K Pay, request, or search…        [▭]  🔔  ◑  net:signet  JD ▾ │
├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  Overview                                          [ ↑ Send ]  [ ↓ Receive ] │
│                                                                              │
│ ┌─ Balance · Org (per network — never summed) ─┐ ┌─ Node health (read-only)─┐│
│ │  signet   ₿ 2.413 71   ≈ $— (testnet)        │ │ ● Connected  LND v0.18   ││
│ │  mainnet  ₿ 0.000 00                          │ │ Inbound  ▓▓▓░░ 1.84 BTC  ││
│ │  ── Spendable now $— · Arriving soon $— ↘ ── │ │ Outbound ▓▓░░░ 0.92 BTC  ││
│ └──────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │ Peers 14/14 · Chans 22   ││
│                                                    │ ⚠ 1 alert: chan 3f… down ││
│ ┌─ Needs action ──────────────────────────────┐  └──────────────────────────┘│
│ │ ⛓ 2 on-chain deposits unconfirmed   [view]  │  ┌─ Accounts (top by bal) ──┐ │
│ │ ✗ 3 Lightning payments failed (route)[view] │  │ Acme-Treasury ₿1.90 admin│ │
│ │ 🔑 1 API key unused 90d  → revoke?          │  │ Payroll       ₿0.31 ●    │ │
│ │ 🤖 agent data-buyer at 99% budget   [view]  │  │ POS-Store-01  ₿0.08 ●    │ │
│ └─────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │ 🤖 agent-bolt  ₿0.02 pol │ │
│                                                    │ + 8 more       [All →]   │ │
│ ┌─ Flow · income vs expense (all rails) ───────┐  └──────────────────────────┘ │
│ │ ▁▂▅▇▆▃▂▄▆█▅▃  LN ▓ on-chain ▒ internal ░  [7d][30d][90d][YTD] │            │
│ └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
│ ┌─ Recent activity (org-wide, unified) ────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│ │ time   account       dir rail     amount    status     by        │        │ │
│ │ 14:02  Payroll       ↑   ⚡LN     −0.0021   settled    alice@key  │        │ │
│ │ 13:58  POS-Store-01  ↓   ⛓chain  +0.0500   1/3 conf   —          │        │ │
│ │ 13:30  agent-bolt    ↑   ⚡L402   −0.0003   settled    🤖 agent    │  [→]   │ │
│ └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Sub-account holder / cashier** (same component, `minRender:'compact'`, empty areas absent):

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ ⊞ My wallet ▾     ⌘K            ▭  ◑  signet  │
├────┬─────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ ⌂  │   Your money                            │
│ ≡  │   ₿ 0.0042   ·   ≈ $—                    │
│ ⊙  │   Spendable now · 1 arriving soon ↘     │
│    │  ┌──────────────┐  ┌──────────────┐     │
│Send│  │  ↑  Send     │  │  ↓  Receive  │     │
│Recv│  └──────────────┘  └──────────────┘     │
│Acty│   People  (Ana)(Lee)(Shop)(Mom)  +      │
│    │  ┌─ Activity ──────────────────────┐    │
│ ⊙  │  │ ↓ +12,000 ⚡  Ana       2m       │    │
│Iden│  │ ↑ −4,500  ⚡  Coffee     14m      │    │
│    │  │ ↓ +5,000  ⚡  Refund    Mon      │    │
│    │  └─────────────────────────────────┘    │
│ No ACCOUNTS / OBSERVE / BUILD: areas absent. │
└────┴─────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Notes:** ONE balance kills the `BalanceOverview` + `CurrentBalance` redundancy (`wallet-view.tsx:79/111`). Balance is the active network's, summed across accounts in scope (safe — single network). "Spendable now / Arriving soon" is the *only* place on-chain leaks onto Home, in plain money-states. "Needs action" (grafted from D2) is the operator's reason to log in — every item is derivable from data we have today (unconfirmed on-chain, failed payments, `ApiKey.expires_at` staleness; the agent-budget item is `flag:agents`). Rail glyphs are low-contrast whispers, not a column.

### 3.2 Unified Send (drawer — one recipient field)

```
┌─ Send money ──────────────────────────────────────────────── ✕ ┐
│ From   [ Acme-Treasury ▾ ]            spendable  ₿ 1.9021         │
│                                                                  │
│ To                                                               │
│ ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────┐  [▭ scan]      │
│ │ paste invoice / @name / LNURL / bc1 address   │               │
│ └──────────────────────────────────────────────┘               │
│ Recent:  (Ana)  (Lee)  (Coffee Bar)  (Mom)                       │
│                                                                  │
│ ── LIGHTNING CASE — rail stays silent (instant, sub-sat) ──      │
│ ▸ Detected: Ana · ana@swissknife.app  ✓  ⚡                      │
│                                                                  │
│ Amount  ┌───────────────┐  ⇄ sats   [25%][50%][Max]             │
│         │ $ 120.00       │  ≈ 161,000 sat                        │
│ Note    [ Dinner split ............................. ]           │
│                                                                  │
│ ── policy check (flag:policy — hidden until backend) ──          │
│ ✓ within Acme daily ceiling · ⚠ >₿0.05 needs 1 co-signer        │
│                                                                  │
│ ┌─ Review ─────────────────────────────────────────────────┐    │
│ │ Send to Ana                                  $120.00      │    │
│ │ Network fee        included · instant         < $0.01     │    │
│ │ Arrives                                       now ⚡      │    │
│ └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘    │
│ ▸ Technical: input, idempotency-key, as cURL/SDK   [ ⌄ ]         │
│                  [   Slide / hold to send  →   ]                 │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

── ON-CHAIN CASE — rail surfaces (fee + speed change the decision) ──
 ▸ Detected: Bitcoin address  bc1q…7f3   ⛓ (on-chain)
 Amount  ┌───────────────┐  ⇄ sats
         │ $ 250.00       │  ≈ 335,000 sat
 ┌─ Review ─────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
 │ Send on-chain to bc1q…7f3                    $250.00      │
 │ Network fee   ( • Economy ~$0.40 )( Standard )( Fast )    │
 │ Arrives       ~30–60 min (1 conf)             ⛓          │
 └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Notes:** ONE field maps 1:1 to the existing `pay({input})` / `walletPay` endpoint (`confirm-payment-dialog.tsx:104/107`), which already takes a generic `input` string and **routes both Lightning and on-chain today** (the "not yet supported" doc string on the input is stale — remove it). The client classifier only *names* the rail to decide disclosure: Lightning stays silent; a detected BTC address surfaces the fee-tier + ETA + reuse panel — the only place a fee selector appears, per the rail-on-three-triggers law. Spending-policy preflight is a v0.3.0 `flag`; the collapsed `▸ Technical` cURL/SDK disclosure ships now. Slide-to-send replaces `ConfirmPaymentDialog`.

### 3.3 Unified Receive (drawer — Lightning + on-chain in one flow)

**Frontend-composed unified QR (v2 correction).** The fused BIP21 QR is **display-only** and ships in Phase 1: the client composes `bitcoin:<addr>?amount=…&lightning=<bolt11>` from the on-chain address (BTC-address endpoint) + the bolt11 (`generateInvoice`) it already holds — no backend leg on the invoice required. "Any" shows the single unified code; segmented LN-only / on-chain-only options remain for payer wallets that don't grok BIP21.

```
┌─ Receive money ───────────────────────────────────────────── ✕ ┐
│ Into   [ POS-Store-01 ▾ ]                                        │
│                                                                  │
│ Rail   ( • Any )   ( ⚡ Lightning )   ( ⛓ On-chain )             │
│        Any = show both: a Lightning invoice + a fresh address    │
│                                                                  │
│ Amount (optional) ┌──────────────┐ ⇄ $   For [ Invoice #3021 ]   │
│                   │ $ 0.00        │                              │
│              ┌───────────────────────────┐                       │
│              │  ██ ▄▄ ██  ▄█ ██ ▄  ██ ██ │   ⚡ Lightning invoice │
│              │  ██  ▄ ▄█ ▄▄  ██▄ ██  ▄█  │   lnbc500u1p…q9x       │
│              │  ▄█ ██ ▄  ██ ▄█  ▄ ██ ██  │   [Copy] [Share] [QR↧] │
│              └───────────────────────────┘   expires 23:41        │
│                                                                  │
│ ⛓ On-chain address (same wallet)                                 │
│   bc1q…m4   ◦ unused · derivation #7    [Copy] [QR] [↻ fresh]    │
│                                                                  │
│ ⚠ This on-chain address was used before — generate a fresh one ↻ │
│   (shown only when BtcAddress.used === true)                     │
│                                                                  │
│ ▸ Or use your identity:  ⚡ store@swiss.knife   🔑 npub…(NIP-05)  │
│ Status: ◔ waiting for payment…              ▸ webhook this (flag) │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

"Any" renders ONE unified BIP21 QR (bolt11 + on-chain address),
composed on the client; the payer's wallet auto-picks the rail.
Shippable in Phase 1 — no backend change needed.
```

**Notes:** Fixes "Receive Bitcoin = Lightning only" head-on. Lightning leg = existing `generateInvoice`/`newWalletInvoice` (`new-invoice-form.tsx` — and we **stop discarding** `bitcoin_output` at line 127). On-chain leg = real generate/list BTC-address endpoints; the **`BtcAddress.used` reuse warning + one-tap fresh address** is the only proactive rail nuance on receive, and it's shippable today. Merchant mode renders this full-screen with a numpad-first amount entry. Replaces both the inline "Receive Bitcoin" card and the duplicate `new-invoice-dialog`.

### 3.4 Activity — unified ledger (three lenses over one timeline)

```
┌─ Activity ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ [⊞ Org ▾]   Lens: ( • Money )  ( Requests* )  ( Webhooks* )   *flag          │
│ Chips:  All · In · Out · Pending    |   rail:[any▾]  account:[any▾]  [date▾]  │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ time    account       dir  rail      amount        status      by      memo  │
│ 14:02   Payroll       ↑    ⚡LN      −0.0021       settled     alice@k  vendor│
│ 13:58   POS-Store-01  ↓    ⛓chain   +0.0500       1/3 conf    —        sale  │
│ 13:41   Acme-Treas    ↑    ⚡LN      −0.1180       settled     ⌘ you    —     │
│ 13:30   agent-bolt    ↑    ⚡L402    −0.0003       settled     🤖 agent api.x │
│ …                                                            [export CSV]    │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

Row detail (a surface, not a modal):
┌─ Coffee Bar — Sent ───────────────────────────────────────── ✕ ┐
│ − $4.50   ·   ≈ 6,120 sats        Paid · today 09:11            │
│ ── Details ──                                                    │
│ Sent over     Lightning ⚡ (instant)                            │
│ Network fee   < $0.01            To  coffeebar@btcpay.shop       │
│ By            ⌘ you              Note —                          │
│ ▸ Technical                                              [ ⌄ ]   │
│    payment_hash · preimage · txid · ledger(Lightning/Onchain/   │
│    Internal) · raw bolt11 · [Copy as cURL] [Copy as bolt402]    │
│ [ Pay again ]                    [ Add Coffee Bar to People ]    │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Notes:** Collapses **four nav items** (wallet+admin Payments+Invoices) into one ledger merging Lightning/On-chain/Internal — the `Ledger` enum and these fields are real. The **`by` column** (D2/D4: human / named key / 🤖 agent, sourced from key metadata today) gives provenance now and audit later; filter-to-one-agent is the operator's kill-switch entry point. **Money** lens ships day one; **Requests/Webhooks** lenses are `flag`'d (Judge consensus: no per-request telemetry exists; "Requests" would be reconstructed from ledger + key metadata until the server ships a real log). Progressive **Technical** disclosure (D1+D4) serves cashier and integrator from one row.

### 3.5 Identity Hub (LN address + Nostr + on-chain addresses)

```
┌─ Identity — how people can pay you ───────────────────────────── ✕? ┐
│ [⊞ Account: POS-Store-01 ▾]                                         │
│ Tabs:  ( ⚡ Lightning Address )  ( 🔑 Nostr )  ( ⛓ On-chain )  ( People )│
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ ⚡ Lightning Address                                                  │
│    store01@swiss.knife          [Copy] [QR] [Share]    ✎ edit        │
│    Reusable · no invoice needed · works for any payer                │
│ ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────   │
│ 🔑 Nostr (NIP-05)   npub1…  ·  verified ✓        [Copy]              │
│ ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────   │
│ ⛓ On-chain receive addresses                  read:btc_address       │
│    ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐     │
│    │ bc1q…m4   unused   #7   created 2d   [Copy][QR]           │     │
│    │ bc1q…9z   ⚠ used   #6   created 9d   [Copy][QR][delete]   │     │
│    └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘     │
│    [ ↻ Generate fresh address ]  (write:btc_address)                 │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Notes:** Merges `wallet/lightning-address` + `wallet/nostr-address` + on-chain (today absent from UI) into one hub. Each tab independently gated; write-perm reveals edit/generate. On-chain tab is the real generate/list/delete BTC-address CRUD with `used` badges. Admin/Org scope shows the cross-wallet version (replaces `admin/lightning-addresses`).

### 3.6 Accounts (RBAC) — governance surface

```
┌─ Scope: Acme-Treasury ▾ ──────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Summary | Members & roles | ▸Spending policy◂(flag) | Credentials | Identity│
│                                                                            │
│ ┌─ SPENDING POLICY  (flag:policy — read-only until backend) ───────────┐   │
│ │ Daily ceiling    [ 0.50 ] BTC   used today ₿0.118 ▓▓░░░░░░░░          │   │
│ │ Per-tx ceiling   [ 0.05 ] BTC                                        │   │
│ │ Approval > [0.05] BTC needs [1 ▾] co-signer                          │   │
│ │ Allowed rails  [✓]Lightning [✓]On-chain [✓]Internal                  │   │
│ │ Allowed payees ( • Anyone ) ( Contacts ) ( Allowlist )               │   │
│ └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                                                                            │
│ ┌─ MEMBERS & ROLES ───────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│ │ member            scopes (flat — real today)        last active      │   │
│ │ you               read:* write:*                     now             │   │
│ │ alice@acme        write:transaction read:wallet      2h ago          │   │
│ │ 🤖 agent-bolt402  write:transaction (≤₿0.001/tx*)    12s ago    ⏻    │   │
│ │ [+ Invite member]  [+ Issue API key]  [+ Add agent (flag)]           │   │
│ └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Honesty fix (all judges):** The `Permission` enum is **flat resource scopes with no per-account grants and no policy/ceiling/approval objects** (`types.gen.ts:742`). So **Phase 1 roles are flat scope chips** (the real model); the per-tx ceiling and co-signer approval render **read-only behind `flag:policy`** and never claim enforcement the server can't deliver. Agents and humans share **one Members table** (D2) with the **`⏻` Freeze kill-switch** (D4) — which binds to the *real* ApiKey revoke endpoint today; "halt spend" is the only net-new server piece.

### 3.7 Node health (read-only observability)

```
┌─ Node health · read-only (never channel management) ──────── read:ln_node ─┐
│ ● Connected   LND v0.18.3   uptime 23d   alias: swiss-node                 │
│ ┌─ Liquidity ─────────────────┐ ┌─ Connectivity ──────────────────────┐   │
│ │ Inbound   ▓▓▓░░  1.84 BTC    │ │ Peers      14 / 14 online            │   │
│ │ Outbound  ▓▓░░░  0.92 BTC    │ │ Channels   22 active · 1 inactive    │   │
│ │ Local/Remote balance bars    │ │ Sync       ✓ chain synced            │   │
│ └─────────────────────────────┘ └──────────────────────────────────────┘   │
│ ┌─ Alerts ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│ │ ⚠ channel 3f2a… inactive 6h    ⚠ outbound liquidity low (<10%)       │   │
│ └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
│ ┌─ Volume (node-wide analytics, all rails) ───────────────────────────┐   │
│ │ ▁▂▅▇▆▃▂▄▆█  payments ▓ invoices ▒    [7d][30d][90d]                  │   │
│ └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Notes:** Renames "Node Management" → "Node health" and reframes today's volume-only `node-view.tsx` as **one card within** an observability page. Liquidity/connectivity/channel-summary/alerts are **net-new server reads** (`flag` the cards that lack endpoints; the Volume card ships today since it already exists). **No channel management, ever** — node-agnosticism is the moat.

### 3.8 Developers / Agents (keys, webhooks, L402, agent-accounts)

```
┌─ Agent · data-buyer ───────────────────────── 🟠 near cap   ⏻ Freeze ─┐ (flag:agents)
│ Wallet ag_4f2 · Key sk_live_…7e2 · created 12d ago                    │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Budget         Policy                  Spend (this period)            │
│ 50,000 sat/mo  rail: L402 only         ▇▇▇▇▇▇▇▇▇░ 49,500 / 50,000      │
│ resets in 6d   max/call 2,000 sat      132 calls · avg 375 sat        │
│                allow: api.x.com        ⚠ 2 calls bounced (402↻)       │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Scopes (key — flat, REAL today): write:transaction read:wallet        │
│      [ edit scopes ]   [ rotate key ]   [ raise budget (flag) ]       │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Live spend (Activity, agent-filtered)                                 │
│ 14:02 ⚡L402 api.x.com/data  402→200  375 sat 88ms                     │
│ [ stream → ]                         [ ▸ export as bolt402 config ]   │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Notes:** The most operable rendering of "agents-as-accounts" (D4). Everything binds to scopes that **exist today** (`write:transaction`, `read:wallet`) — so pre-0.3.0 it **degrades cleanly to "wallet + scoped key + agent-filtered Activity."** Budget/policy/L402-bounce render behind `flag`. The **`⏻` Freeze** (revoke key + halt) is the recurring blast-radius primitive. **API keys** page ships today (real `/me/api-keys`, `expires_at`, scopes, bulk revoke from `admin/api-keys`); **Webhooks** and **L402 endpoints** are `flag`'d.

---

## 4. The unified money model — the rail-on-three-triggers law

The rail (Lightning vs on-chain) is **a derived property, never a user-chosen primary input.** It is allowed to surface on **exactly three triggers, and nowhere else**:

| Trigger | Where it surfaces | What the user sees | Backend reality |
|---|---|---|---|
| **1. Fee** | Send Review (on-chain only); on-chain has a non-trivial fee | "Network fee ≈ $1.80 (adjustable ▾)"; LN shows "included · <$0.01" and stays silent | LN + on-chain send both routable today via the unified `input`; fee-tier is a client affordance |
| **2. Speed / finality** | Send Review; Home "Spendable now / Arriving soon"; Activity status (`1/3 conf`) | "Arrives now ⚡" vs "~30–60 min ⛓"; pending on-chain expressed as plain money-states | confirmation status exists on `BtcOutput` |
| **3. Address reuse** | Receive (on-chain leg) | "⚠ used before — generate fresh ↻" | **shippable today** via `BtcAddress.used` |

**Everywhere else, no rail words appear.** This is a hard, testable law — it removes every "should we show the rail here?" debate and is the highest-consensus idea across all four judges.

### How the rails blend per flow

- **Send:** ONE recipient field → backend's unified `input`, which routes **both** Lightning and on-chain today. Client classifies only to *name* the rail and decide disclosure. LN: silent. On-chain: surfaces the fee-tier + ETA + reuse panel.
- **Receive:** ONE surface; "Any" renders a single **frontend-composed BIP21 QR** (bolt11 from `generateInvoice` + address from the BTC-address endpoint), with LN-only / on-chain-only fallbacks. The reuse warning is live now. No backend change needed.
- **Amount entry:** identical for both rails — `number-input` with sats⇄fiat toggle, optional for receive (any-amount), required-or-from-invoice for send. The rail never changes the amount UI.
- **Activity:** one ledger, rail as a whisper glyph + a `rail` filter (off by default); full rail/fee/txid/preimage in the row-detail Technical disclosure.
- **Balance:** one number per network (never summed across regtest/signet/mainnet). On-chain pending shown only as "Arriving soon."

---

## 5. The v0.3.0 L402 / AI-agent-as-account future

The adaptive shell is the cleanest substrate because **an agent is just another point in `(mode × role × scope)`** — it needs zero new IA, only a new `mode` and a generated surface.

1. **Agent = wallet (segregation = blast radius) + scoped API key (auth) + spending policy (RBAC budget).** Provisioning is one flow chaining three existing primitives: create/pick wallet → mint per-wallet API key with a scoped subset → attach Spending Policy. Account segregation *is* blast-radius control; the ceiling *is* the budget. Pre-0.3.0 this degrades to the first two real steps.
2. **`mode='agent'` renders no human chrome** — the same manifest resolves to a generated, read-only **Agent Console** (`surface:'panel'`, `minRender` drops nav): remaining budget, current policy, recent L402 spend, kill-switch. This proves the engine handles a non-human persona without a fork.
3. **L402 needs no new model.** An L402 invoice is just another value the unified Send `input` accepts; the agent's autonomous payment is the *same code path* as a human Send, gated by policy instead of a click. Humans audit it in the same unified Activity (rail badge `⚡L402`, `by = 🤖 agent`) and reconcile in the same Volume view. The 402→200 retry renders as a normal status.
4. **Spending Policy is designed once** and serves both a cashier's daily limit and an agent's spend cap — RBAC-as-budget generalized, not bolted on. Parental controls and agent budget ceilings are literally the same UI.
5. **Honest fences:** `/agents`, policy/budget objects, webhooks, L402-endpoint pricing, and per-request telemetry have **zero backend today** (verified). Every agent surface is `flag`'d and degrades to "wallet + scoped key + agent-filtered Activity." The **`⏻` Freeze** binds to the *real* ApiKey revoke endpoint now; only "halt spend" is net-new. **"Copy as bolt402 snippet"** (D3) on any executed action turns the human flow into living agent documentation — the cleanest agent on-ramp, shippable as a string template on top of the existing request body.

---

## 6. Phased implementation plan (mapped to the current dashboard)

Current state recap: routes under `src/app/(index)/wallet/*` and `src/app/(index)/admin/*`; `components/transactions/*` (send/receive cards + dialogs); `sections/onboarding`; settings drawer; MUI X DataGrids; permission-tagged `nav-config-dashboard.tsx`; `hasAllPermissions`; mock `workspaces-popover`.

### Phase 0 — Foundations (the engine, no visible feature churn)
- **Build:** the `Module` manifest type + a `resolveModules(mode, permissions, scope)` resolver wrapping the existing `hasAllPermissions`. Add `NEXT_PUBLIC_DEPLOYMENT_MODE` (default `server`) to `global-config`. Build the `flag` registry. Build the **"why is this hidden?" inspector**.
- **Rename:** `nav-config-dashboard.tsx` → module manifest; keep routes (deep links + RBAC survive).
- **Delete:** nothing yet.
- **Ship:** identical UI, now manifest-driven. De-risks everything downstream.

### Phase 1 — Unify money flows (the highest-leverage, lowest-risk win)
- **Build:** unified **Send drawer** (ONE field → `pay`/`walletPay`; routes **both LN and on-chain**; on-chain shows fee-tier + ETA + reuse) and unified **Receive drawer** (frontend-composed **BIP21 unified QR** + LN-only/on-chain-only fallbacks; on-chain address via BTC-address endpoint + `used` reuse warning). Encode the **rail-on-three-triggers** classifier.
- **Merge:** `new-payment-card` + `new-payment-dialog` + `new-payment-form` → Send drawer. `new-invoice-card` + `new-invoice-dialog` + `new-invoice-form` → Receive drawer.
- **Delete:** all `components/transactions/*-dialog.tsx` modals; the inline Quick Transfer + "Receive Bitcoin" cards in `wallet-view.tsx`; the Lightning-only `qr-dialog` default title.
- **Ship:** on-chain receive (the biggest missing capability) + no more modal-vs-card duplication.

### Phase 2 — Merge surfaces + one Home
- **Build:** unified **Activity** page (Money lens, `by` column, Technical row-detail surface, rail filter). One **balance** panel. Manifest-driven **Home grid** (`WalletView` maps panel-modules). Make `workspaces-popover` the **real scope switcher** (per-network, never summed).
- **Merge:** `wallet/payments` + `admin/payments` + `wallet/invoices` + `admin/invoices` → `/activity` (scoped). `current-balance` + `balance-overview` → one panel.
- **Delete:** `CurrentBalance` redundancy; the `WALLET`/`ADMINISTRATION` two-group split.
- **Ship:** the redundancy bugs are gone; the adaptive Home is live.

### Phase 3 — Identity, Accounts, Node, Developers
- **Build:** **Identity Hub** (merge LN/Nostr/on-chain tabs). **Accounts** with flat-scope Members table + **`⏻` Freeze** (real revoke). **Node health** page (Volume ships; liquidity/connectivity `flag`'d pending server reads). **API keys** page; "Copy as cURL/bolt402" affordance. **"Needs Action"** Home pane.
- **Merge:** `wallet/lightning-address` + `wallet/nostr-address` + `admin/lightning-addresses` → Identity Hub. `admin/api-keys` → BUILD.
- **Rename:** `admin/lightning-node` → Node health + Volume.
- **Ship:** the full operator cockpit on real backend only.

### Phase 4 — v0.3.0 agent surfaces + fused QR (backend-gated)
- **Build (as the v0.3.0 backend lands):** Agent detail + Agent Console (`mode='agent'`); Spending Policies (read→write when policy objects land); Webhooks; L402 endpoints; Requests/Webhooks Activity lenses.
- **Ship:** the agent platform, incrementally, never ahead of the server.

### Backend asks (to unblock fenced features), in priority order
_Some of these may already be tracked or scoped to v0.3.0 — check before filing; don't open duplicates._
1. **Cleanup:** remove the stale "on-chain not supported" doc string on `SendPaymentRequest.input` (on-chain send already works).
2. Node read endpoints (liquidity, peers, channel summary, alerts) → unlocks deep Node health (binary connected + client-computed Volume ship today).
3. Spending-policy / budget objects (v0.3.0 agent work) → unlocks policy enforcement.
4. Per-request telemetry / API request log → unlocks the Requests lens + agent call metrics.
5. Actor attribution (created_by / api-key id) on Payment/Invoice → unlocks the Activity `by` column + agent-filtered audit.
6. Agent + webhook + L402-endpoint objects (v0.3.0) → unlocks the agent platform.

---

## 7. Open questions and risks

1. **Fused-QR timing.** Phase 1 ships honest two-code receive; the single-QR upgrade is purely cosmetic-positive and strictly depends on backend ask #1. Risk: low — degrades cleanly.
2. **On-chain send is real (v2).** The first draft wrongly fenced it off a stale doc string; the unified `input` routes on-chain today (just delete the comment). No dead-path needed. Its only genuine work is on-chain fee/confirmation UX polish (see Appendix A, Gap 5).
3. **Policy enforcement gap.** The flat `Permission` model has no ceilings/approvals. Risk: showing editable policy implies enforcement the server can't deliver. Mitigated by `flag:policy` rendering **read-only** until ask #4. This is the single most important honesty guardrail.
4. **Scope-switcher ambiguity.** "Move money from the wrong account" is a real failure mode. The current scope must be **always-visible and unmissable** in the top bar and echoed in every Send/Receive "From/Into" field. Needs usability validation.
5. **Balance is single-network (v2).** The connected node defines the network, so the live balance is one number — no cross-network summing machinery, no per-network side-by-side. The first draft over-engineered this. Cross-network care applies only to *historical* records that may span networks.
6. **Composition debuggability.** "Why can't I see X?" is now a 3-axis function. The inspector (§2.5) is **mandatory, not optional**, or support cost balloons. Plus a `(mode × canonical-role)` render test matrix.
7. **Permission gate is convenience, never security.** The backend must remain the single source of truth; a wrong `requires[]` is a UX bug, and the server must reject unauthorized calls regardless.
8. **Compact-nav "looks broken."** A cashier's 4-item rail risks reading as empty. Mitigated by the mini icon rail + never rendering empty area headers; validate with the cashier persona.
9. **Command bar scope.** We deliberately do NOT build a full fuzzy verb+noun+arg engine this cycle (Judge consensus: the existing `searchbar` is nav-only; a real engine is the costliest subsystem in the brief). We extend ⌘K incrementally to *launch* Send/Receive and jump to entities. Full command grammar is a post-Phase-4 stretch.
10. **i18n/a11y.** Every new module title, rail-disclosure string, and "Needs Action" item needs locale keys and screen-reader treatment for the low-contrast rail glyphs.

---

**Recommendation:** Adopt this RFC. Start Phase 0 + Phase 1 immediately — they are pure frontend, lean entirely on verified-existing backend, and deliver the two biggest wins (on-chain receive + de-duplicated money flows) with zero backend dependency. Everything aspirational is fenced behind `flag`s that flip on as the six backend asks land, so the dashboard ships continuous value and never promises what the server can't do.

Relevant files for implementers (absolute paths):
- `/Users/dario/dev/numeraire/swissknife/dashboard/src/layouts/nav-config-dashboard.tsx` (→ module manifest)
- `/Users/dario/dev/numeraire/swissknife/dashboard/src/auth/permissions.ts` (`hasAllPermissions` gate)
- `/Users/dario/dev/numeraire/swissknife/dashboard/src/lib/swissknife/types.gen.ts` (Invoice `:331`, BtcAddress `:74`, Permission `:742`, SendPaymentRequest `:797`, NewInvoiceRequest `:627`)
- `/Users/dario/dev/numeraire/swissknife/dashboard/src/components/transactions/` (send/receive cards+dialogs to merge/delete; `new-invoice-form.tsx:127` discards `bitcoin_output`)
- `/Users/dario/dev/numeraire/swissknife/dashboard/src/sections/wallet/view/wallet-view.tsx` (Total/Current redundancy `:79`/`:111`)
- `/Users/dario/dev/numeraire/swissknife/dashboard/src/layouts/components/workspaces-popover.tsx` (mock → real scope switcher)
- `/Users/dario/dev/numeraire/swissknife/dashboard/src/sections/node/view/node-view.tsx` (volume-only → Node health page)
- `/Users/dario/dev/numeraire/swissknife/dashboard/src/layouts/components/searchbar/` (⌘K, nav-only today)

---

# Appendix A — Completeness critique (adversarial pass)

_Auto-generated by the design sprint's critic agent. Treat as the open-items backlog for the RFC above._

> **Editor's note (v2):** the maintainer corrected several premises the critic built on. **Superseded:** Gap 1 (balance is single-network — there is no cross-network summing to be dangerous), the on-chain-send and fused-QR "backend-blocked" framing throughout (both ship in Phase 1 — on-chain send already works, the fused QR is frontend-composed), and Gap 5's "entirely absent" claim (on-chain send works; its fee/RBF/confirmation/dust UX is real *future polish*, worth keeping as a checklist). **Still valid backlog:** empty/error/loading states (Gap 8), Lightning expiry/failure surfaces (Gap 6), the migration/redirect map (Gap 9), Contacts-model thinness (Gap 10), accessibility & mobile (Risks 6–7), and the composition-matrix QA strategy (Risk 8 / Suggestion 11). RBAC spending policy (Gaps 2/11/12) is real but **scoped to v0.3.0 agent work**, not a surprise gap.

## Gaps
1. BALANCE IS NOT PER-NETWORK — the RFC's central correctness claim is unbuildable as written. `Balance` (types.gen.ts:48) is a single flat object (available_msat/reserved_msat/sent_msat/received_msat) and `Wallet`/`WalletOverview` (lines 899/938) carry NO currency or network field. `Currency`/network appears ONLY on individual `Invoice` (:348) and `Payment` (:684) records. So 'Spendable now / Arriving soon' and 'per-network, never summed' Org balance (§3.1, §2.3, §4) cannot be sourced from any balance endpoint; they'd have to be reconstructed by scanning and bucketing every payment/invoice by currency client-side. The RFC asserts this as a shippable Phase-2 correctness guardrail but it is actually a net-new backend ask (balance broken down by Currency). This is the single biggest unflagged gap.
2. THE `by` (actor/provenance) COLUMN HAS NO DATA SOURCE. §3.1, §3.4, §3.8 repeatedly claim the unified Activity `by` column (human / named key / agent) is 'sourced from key metadata today' and 'gives provenance now.' Verified false: `Payment` (:671) and `Invoice` (:331) carry no created_by/api_key_id/actor field, and no list query exposes one. The agent kill-switch entry point, audit story, and 'filter-to-one-agent' all depend on this nonexistent attribution. It is a backend ask, not a ship-now feature — but it is not in the §6 backend-asks list at all.
3. NODE HEALTH HAS ALMOST NO BACKEND. §3.7 draws inbound/outbound liquidity bars, peers 14/14, channels 22 active/1 inactive, sync status, and an alerts pane. The ONLY node data in the API is `HealthCheck` (:281): {database, is_healthy, ln_provider} — three coarse HealthStatus values. There is no liquidity, peer, channel, uptime, alias, or alert data anywhere. The RFC says only 'liquidity/connectivity cards are flagged' but in reality EVERYTHING except a binary connected/disconnected dot is missing, and even the 'Volume card ships today' is misleading: there is no volume/analytics endpoint — node-view computes it client-side from payment/invoice lists. The node-health page is ~90% aspirational, not ~40%.
4. ACTIVITY VIEW LACKS DATE-RANGE AND CURRENCY FILTERING SERVER-SIDE. ListPayments/ListInvoices queries (:3170) expose only status, ledger, ln_addresses, btc_addresses, ids, limit/offset, order_direction — NO from/to date range and NO currency/network filter. The RFC's Activity chips ('[date▾]', the Home '[7d][30d][90d][YTD]' flow chart, per-network bucketing) and the wallet-view's existing 'date and ledger filters' imply server filtering that does not exist; everything must be client-side over a paginated list, which breaks for any non-trivial history. No pagination/virtualization strategy is described for the unified ledger.
5. ON-CHAIN SEND EDGE CASES ARE ENTIRELY ABSENT (beyond 'coming soon'). The reviewer brief explicitly asks about fee selection, RBF/CPFP, confirmation states, dust, and change — the RFC addresses NONE of these for the future on-chain send. It mentions a 'fee-tier + ETA + reuse panel' as a one-line future, but no design for: fee-rate selection UX (sat/vB vs presets), RBF opt-in/bump flow, CPFP, dust-limit rejection and minimum-amount handling, change-address behavior, replaceability signaling, or what confirmation target maps to which ETA. BtcOutputStatus even includes IMMATURE (:183) — coinbase-style immature outputs — which has zero UI treatment. These need at least a fenced design sketch so the Phase-4 panel isn't reinvented.
6. LIGHTNING FAILURE/EXPIRY/MPP EDGE CASES ARE UNDER-SPECIFIED. InvoiceStatus has EXPIRED (:413) and LnInvoice carries expires_at/expiry (:487) but the Receive flow shows only a static 'expires 23:41' — no design for expiry countdown, auto-regeneration on expiry, or the EXPIRED terminal state. Send shows a happy path only: no design for route-not-found / no-liquidity / timeout failures (the `Payment.error` field at :692 exists and is unused in any mockup), no partial/MPP or stuck-pending (PaymentStatus.PENDING) handling, and explicitly NO Lightning→on-chain fallback even though Direction-1's 'fallback to on-chain' is in the brief. The 'Needs action' pane lists 'Lightning payments failed (route)' but no flow lets the user act on it.
7. ADDRESS-REUSE WARNING LOGIC IS INCOMPLETE. The reuse warning keys on `BtcAddress.used` (:98), but the design never says what happens to the OLD address (does receiving on a 'used' address still work? is it deleted?), how 'fresh' interacts with the existing list (gap-limit / unbounded address generation — a real wallet-recovery concern), or what the `address_type` choice (P2WPKH vs P2TR, :108) means to a user. NewBtcAddressRequest takes an optional `type` (:1543) — no UI decides or surfaces it. Dust on receive (a payer sending sub-dust on-chain) is unaddressed.
8. EMPTY / ERROR / LOADING STATES ARE NEARLY ABSENT FROM THE DESIGN. Every mockup shows a populated happy path. There is no design for: first-run/zero-balance Home, zero-transaction Activity, zero-wallet Org scope, API 401/403/500 (ErrorResponse :267 is {reason} — how is `reason` surfaced?), 503 from /system/ready, network-offline, slow-list skeletons, or the 'coming soon' on-chain block's interaction with a paste that is a VALID bc1 address vs garbage. The composition engine's failure mode (a module whose endpoint 500s) is undefined — does the area vanish, error-card, or retry?
9. MIGRATION RISK IS UNDERWEIGHTED. §6 says 'keep routes (deep links + RBAC survive)' but the redesign deletes /wallet/payments, /wallet/invoices, /admin/payments, /admin/invoices and merges LN/Nostr/onchain pages — these ARE route deletions. No redirect map, no bookmark/deep-link preservation plan, no plan for in-flight users mid-session, and no feature-flag/rollback strategy for shipping the manifest engine (Phase 0) against the live two-group nav. 'De-risks everything' is asserted, not shown.
10. CONTACTS MODEL IS LIGHTNING-ONLY AND THIN — contradicts 'one identity hub / People across rails.' `Contact` (:211) is just {ln_address, contact_since}: no name/label, no on-chain address, no Nostr, no avatar. The mockups show '(Ana)(Lee)(Shop)(Mom)' named, on-chain-capable contacts and 'Add Coffee Bar to People' — none of which the model supports. Contacts as a cross-rail recipient book is a backend ask not listed in §6.
11. AGENT/L402 READINESS HAS NO CONCRETE PRIMITIVES. §5 leans on 'wallet + scoped key + policy,' but there is no spending-policy object, no budget/period/reset field, no L402 endpoint object, no webhook object, and no per-request telemetry anywhere in the API (correctly fenced) — yet the Agent Console mockup (§3.8) shows budget bars, '402→200' retries, calls/avg-sat, and 'bounced 402' counters as if reconstructable. They are not reconstructable from Payment records (no L402/HTTP-status/latency fields). Even the degraded 'wallet + scoped key + agent-filtered Activity' fallback fails because Activity can't filter by agent (no actor field). The agent story degrades to LESS than the RFC claims.
12. MULTI-TENANT / RBAC GRANULARITY GAP. Permissions are a flat global scope set on a JWT/key (:742) — they are NOT per-wallet grants. So the scope switcher's 'Account scope' and the Members-per-account table (§3.6) imply per-account role assignment the backend cannot express: a key with write:transaction can act on ANY wallet it can address via wallet_id, not a specific one. The RFC calls roles 'flat scope chips (the real model)' but still renders a per-account Members table, which misrepresents the actual blast radius. How sub-account isolation is actually enforced (one user_id per wallet? JWT subject mapping?) is never pinned down.

## Risks
1. Honesty-by-construction is the RFC's stated core law, yet the three flagship operator surfaces (per-network balance, the `by` provenance column, and Node health liquidity) are each presented as ship-now or lightly-flagged when all three are substantially net-new backend. This is the same 'UI implies a capability the server can't fulfill' failure the RFC was written to prevent — concentrated in exactly the cockpit that sells the product to the operator persona.
2. The merchant persona is the most under-served despite being a named deployment mode. 'Charge' full-screen numpad, POS plugin, per-till wallets, and 'Settlements' are mentioned but never designed; merchant on-chain settlement (the common BTCPay use case) is blocked by the missing on-chain send, and refunds (a core merchant flow) appear nowhere. Merchant is arguably the persona with the largest gap between brief ambition and verified capability.
3. The Desktop persona is essentially undesigned beyond 'OS window chrome + global ⌘K' — no offline behavior, no secure-key-storage story, no auto-update, no deep-link/protocol-handler (bitcoin:/lightning:) handling, which is the single most valuable desktop affordance and ties directly to the unified-send classifier.
4. Per-network non-summing is presented as a hard correctness constraint (matches the codebase memory that one instance is reused across regtest/signet/mainnet) — but since the balance object has no network dimension, a naive Phase-2 implementation will silently SUM across networks, producing exactly the dangerous wrong number the memory warns against. The risk is not 'denser UI' (as the RFC frames it) but 'incorrect, fund-loss-adjacent balance' until the backend ask lands.
5. Scope-switcher mis-send risk is acknowledged but the always-visible network badge it relies on is itself unbuildable per-balance (see balance gap), so the primary mitigation for 'move money from the wrong account/network' is weaker than claimed.
6. Accessibility is reduced to one line ('screen-reader treatment for low-contrast rail glyphs'). Slide/hold-to-send (§3.2) is a serious a11y and motor-impairment hazard with no keyboard/AT-accessible alternative specified; the dark-only theme has no stated contrast-ratio audit; QR-only receive states need text alternatives; the compact icon rail risks unlabeled icon-only nav.
7. Mobile is asserted ('full-screen sheets') but never designed: numpad amount entry, sheet dismissal vs accidental send, QR scanner permissions/failure, and the data-dense DataGrid Activity table on a phone (DataGrids are notoriously poor on mobile) are all unaddressed despite mobile being implied for cashier/merchant.
8. The composition engine multiplies the QA surface to (5 modes x 4+ roles x 2 scopes x N flag states) — the RFC asks for a render test matrix but provides no strategy for combinatorial explosion, snapshot drift, or how the 'why is this hidden?' inspector stays in sync with real backend 403s (a module can pass all three client gates and still 403).

## Suggestions
1. Reclassify three features from ship-now to backend-blocked and add them to the §6 ask list with priority: (a) balance broken down by Currency/network on /v1/me/balance and /wallets/overviews (unblocks per-network balance + the always-visible network badge); (b) actor attribution (created_by / api_key_id / key_name) on Payment and Invoice plus an actor filter on the list queries (unblocks the `by` column, agent-filtered Activity, and the kill-switch entry point); (c) date-range + currency filters on ListPayments/ListInvoices (unblocks Activity date chips and the Home flow chart). Until (a) lands, Phase 2 must show ONE balance with an explicit 'across all networks' caveat or block Org-scope balance entirely — never a silently-summed number.
2. Replace the §3.7 Node health mockup with an honest two-state design for Phase 1: a connectivity card driven by the real HealthCheck {is_healthy, database, ln_provider} only, plus the existing client-computed Volume card. Move ALL liquidity/peer/channel/alert cards behind flag:node-health and add 'node read endpoints (liquidity, peers, channel summary, sync, alias, alerts)' as a single explicit backend ask — the current RFC understates how much is missing.
3. Add a dedicated 'States' section covering, for every flow and the manifest engine: empty (zero wallet / zero tx / first-run), loading (skeletons), and error (map ErrorResponse.reason to toast/inline; 401->re-auth, 403->'why hidden?' inspector deep-link, 500/503->retry card, offline). Define what a module does when its endpoint fails (recommend: error-card in place, never vanish, to distinguish 'absent by design' from 'broken').
4. Add a Lightning-edge-cases subsection: expiry countdown + auto-regenerate on the Receive invoice, EXPIRED terminal UI, and a Send failure surface that reads Payment.error (:692) and offers retry / try-different-route / (future) on-chain fallback. Wire the 'Needs action' failed-payment item to an actual remediation flow rather than a dead 'view'.
5. Add an on-chain-edge-cases subsection (even if entirely fenced behind flag:onchain-send) sketching: fee-rate selection (preset tiers + custom sat/vB), RBF opt-in and bump UI, CPFP-on-receive for stuck incoming, dust-limit and minimum-amount handling, change/UTXO opacity, and a mapping from BtcOutputStatus (Unconfirmed/Confirmed/Spent/Immature, :178) to user-facing money-states. IMMATURE in particular needs a defined string today since it can already appear on received outputs.
6. Pin down the real RBAC/multi-tenant model before designing the Members table. Confirm whether wallets map 1:1 to user_id (JWT subject) and how a scoped key is constrained to a wallet (wallet_id on every request vs server-side binding). Then design §3.6 to the ACTUAL isolation boundary; if per-account role grants don't exist, the Members table should be reframed as 'keys/JWTs that can address this wallet' rather than implying per-account RBAC.
7. Add a concrete migration plan: a route-redirect map for every deleted/merged route (/wallet/payments + /admin/payments -> /activity?scope=, etc.), a feature-flag to run the manifest engine in parallel with the legacy nav, a rollback path, and a deep-link/bookmark preservation note. State explicitly which Phase is reversible.
8. Strengthen the Contacts->People story: list the backend asks needed (label/name, on-chain address, Nostr key on Contact; an add/update contact endpoint) since the current model is {ln_address, contact_since} only. Until then, scope 'People' to LN-address contacts and don't show on-chain/named contacts in mockups.
9. Add a real accessibility + mobile section: provide a keyboard/AT-accessible alternative to slide-to-send (e.g., a confirm-button with a hold-to-confirm option), commit to a WCAG AA contrast audit of the dark theme, require text/copy alternatives beside every QR, label the compact icon rail, and design the phone layout for Send/Receive sheets and the Activity list (card list instead of DataGrid on mobile).
10. Design the merchant and desktop personas to parity with operator/cashier: a merchant Charge (numpad) + Settlements + Refund flow (note refund is blocked on on-chain send / needs a Lightning-refund path), and a desktop protocol-handler (bitcoin:/lightning:) + secure-storage + auto-update note. Add a refund flow generally — it's absent across the whole RFC.
11. Add a QA strategy for the composition matrix: a canonical fixture per (mode x role) with snapshot tests, a contract that the 'why is this hidden?' inspector also surfaces server-side 403s (client gates can pass while the server still rejects), and an explicit statement that the client manifest is convenience-only with the server as sole authority (already stated as a principle — make it a testable invariant).
12. Correct the agent-degradation claim in §5: without an actor field, 'agent-filtered Activity' is impossible today, so the honest pre-0.3.0 fallback is 'a segregated wallet + a scoped key' only. State that, and make actor-attribution (suggestion 1b) a prerequisite for ANY agent-observability surface, not a flag-flip nicety.
